title: • What structure does it follow?
content:
• What structure does it follow?
3. Rewrite a scene from it in your own style.

6. Back-and-Forth Brainstorming (Email Process)

Instead of working alone, let’s email ideas back and forth to refine them.

How This Works
1. You send me an email with:
• A rough idea.
• A question or problem (e.g., “My character feels flat” or “I don’t know what happens next”).
2. I reply with:
• Ideas, clarifications, and challenges.
• A short writing prompt to spark momentum.
3. You respond with a new draft or idea.
4. We repeat the cycle until it feels solid.

Final Takeaways

✅ Stories are about change. If a character isn’t making choices, they’re stuck.
✅ Scenes must shift. Every scene should move from one state to another.
✅ Positive & negative pulses drive tension.
✅ The wave structure matters. Stories rise and fall in a rhythmic way.
context: From document: Writing Guide for Derek: Structure, Exploration, and Momentum — chunk 7 (chunk 7/8)
